question_text: Two electromagnetic waves have frequencies of 5 × 10¹⁴ Hz and 5 × 10¹² Hz. Which wave has the longer wavelength and why?
correct_answer: The wave with frequency 5 × 10¹² Hz has the longer wavelength because wavelength and frequency are inversely related. Lower frequency means longer wavelength.
explanation: The relationship is: wavelength = speed ÷ frequency. Since all EM waves travel at the same speed, the one with lower frequency must have a longer wavelength. The wave with 5 × 10¹² Hz has a lower frequency, so it has a longer wavelength.
